mechanism_narrative: A rising nonfarm payrolls level signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positively correlated with month-over-month changes in PAYEMS (Rank IC < 0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, restrictive monetary policy elevates corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates demand expansion validated by supply response, a combination that amplifies earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO changes does not improve prediction of 20-day S&P 500 returns beyond the individual series (incremental R-squared < 0.01) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, accelerating job creation captured by falling unemployment rate triggers a catch-up in consumer spending and corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0% and declines, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in new residential housing starts signals durable demand for construction materials, labor, and credit, which spills over into broad economic activity and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in HOUST show no significant positive correlation with S&P 500 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production relative to recent trend signals deteriorating corporate output and pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 3-month changes in INDPRO coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month acceleration in consumer prices signals mounting inflationary pressure that erodes real corporate earnings and compresses equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4.0%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales during periods of restrictive monetary policy indicate that consumer demand is resilient to higher financing costs, a divergence that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising RSAFS and FEDFUNDS above 3.0% does not produce positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no predictive power (F-test p > 0.05).
